channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The best cozy games during a stressful Thanksgiving season
item.description: For many, video games are loud, violent, and competitive. But for some they are the opposite: quiet, soft, and low-stakes. Welcome to the world of cozy gaming.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Bezos, Musk rivalry heats up ahead of Trump’s second term
item.description: A social media spat between Elon Musk and Jeff Bezos this week underscored a sharpening rivalry between two of the world’s richest men at a time when both are trying to grow their sprawling businesses empires in President-elect Trump’s second term.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, The Hill

item.title: ICYMI: the week's 7 biggest tech stories from Casio's smart ring watch to YouTube’s Spotify Wrapped
item.description: ICYMI here's the biggest tech news stories from Snap, ChatGPT, Casio, and more for November 23, 2024.
item.dc:creator: hamish.hector@futurenet.com (Hamish Hector), TechRadar

item.title: AWS and Anthropic ink deal to accelerate model development, enhance AI chips
item.description: The announcement that Amazon Web Services (AWS) will be Anthropic’s primary training partner confirms rumors of an even tighter partnership between the two companies. They announced Friday that Anthropic will use AWS Trainium processors to train and deploy its Claude family of models.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: SpaceX will deliver its first lunar terrain vehicle to the Moon by 2029
item.description: Hot on the heels of another productive test flight, two new lunar customers have been added to the flight manifest for SpaceX’s Starship. Eagle landing Lunar Outpost said on Thursday that SpaceX will deliver its first Lunar Terrain Vehicle, Eagle, to the Moon by 2029.
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz, Fast Company

item.title: Bitcoin ticks closer to $100,000 in extended surge following US elections
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Bitcoin extended its streak of record highs after ticking above $99,000 for the first time. The cryptocurrency has rocketed more than 40% in just two weeks.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: TypeScript 5.7 arrives with improved error reporting
item.description: TypeScript 5.7, the latest planned release of Microsoft’s JavaScript variant with syntax for types, is now available in a production release. With version 5.7, the language’s type system is now able to report errors when variables have never been initialized.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Bluesky has a growing problem: fake accounts
item.description: On Wednesday afternoon, billionaire activist investor Bill Ackman appeared to post on Bluesky a plan to destroy the burgeoning social media platform.
item.dc:creator: Joe Berkowitz, Fast Company

item.title: Supreme Court allows multibillion-dollar class action to proceed against Meta
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court is allowing a multibillion-dollar class action investors’ lawsuit to proceed against Facebook parent Meta, stemming from the privacy scandal involving the Cambridge Analytica political consulting firm.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: The future according to Google DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis
item.description: Google DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis used to get some of his most fulfilling work done between midnight and 3 a.m. A self-described “nocturnal person,” the Londoner devoted the solitude of his overnight shift to catching up on scientific papers, coming up with new ideas, and just plain thinking.
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: DARPA tries a simple but profound concept to improve cybersecurity
item.description: "This idea of compartmentalization has a realization by breaking systems up into small pieces," said Howard Strobe. The post DARPA tries a simple but profound concept to improve cybersecurity first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: From vision to value: A strategic approach to generative AI adoption
item.description: Generative AI has the potential to redefine productivity, create novel applications, and reinvent customer experience.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: NetBox Labs launches tools to combat network configuration drift
item.description: NetBox Labs is expanding its network management platform this week with a pair of new products designed to tackle the growing challenges of infrastructure documentation and configuration management.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Risk & Compliance Exchange 2024: NIST’s Ron Ross on engineering protections into cyber-physical systems
item.description: The massive convergence of cyber and physical systems has prompted the need for a framework based on engineering principles, the NIST fellow says. The post Risk & Compliance Exchange 2024: NIST’s Ron Ross on engineering protections into cyber-physical systems first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Hypersonic jet promises NYC to London in under an hour
item.description: A Texas company is developing a hypersonic jet, Stargazer, that could fly from New York City to London in under an hour, redefining boundaries of aviation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: CIOs contend with gen AI growing pains
item.description: The road ahead for IT leaders in turning the promise of generative AI into business value remains steep and daunting, but the key components of the gen AI roadmap — data, platform, and skills — are evolving and becoming better defined.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: AWS prepares to command an army of AI agents
item.description: According to a recent report from SNS Insider, the global AI agents market was valued at $3.7 billion in 2023. The market is expected to grow to $103.6 billion by 2032, with a compound annual growth rate of 44.9% during the forecast period from 2024 to 2032.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: In the age of AI, what is a PC? Arm has its answer
item.description: Amid the uncertainty around what makes a Windows 11 PC a Copilot+ PC, and how that differs from an AI PC, Arm is bringing some clarity — or perhaps a new source of confusion — with its definition of what constitutes an Arm PC.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Australia rejects Elon Musk's claim that it plans to control access to the internet
item.description: An Australian Cabinet minister has rejected X Corp. owner Elon Musk’s allegation that the government intends to control all Australians' access to the internet through legislation that would ban young children from social media.
item.dc:creator: Rod Mcguirk, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Los CIO consideran que la gestión de costes puede acabar con el valor de la IA
item.description: Los CIO que sienten la presión de implementar proyectos de inteligencia artificial (IA) exitosos tienen una segunda preocupación: no tienen el presupuesto para llevarlo a cabo.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Emperor penguin released at sea 20 days after waddling onto Australian beach
item.description: The only emperor penguin known to have swum from Antarctica to Australia has been released at sea 20 days after he waddled ashore on a popular tourist beach.
item.dc:creator: Rod Mcguirk, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Thai court dismisses activist's suit against Israeli spyware producer over lack of evidence
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — A Thai court on Thursday threw out a lawsuit brought by a pro-democracy activist which alleged spyware produced by an Israeli tech firm had been used to hack his phone.
item.dc:creator: JERRY HARMER and DAVID RISING, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: CIOs and CMOs: Breaking Down Silos for Better Customer Experiences in 2025
item.description: Breaking down silos between CIOs and CMOs to deliver better customer experiences in 2025.
item.dc:creator: Keith Turco, Fast Company

item.title: Harnessing AI: A NetApp perspective
item.description: We take pride in our heritage at NetApp. Our heritage is rooted in developing innovative solutions that address the challenges of storing, managing, and protecting data in a complex IT environment.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: MLB will test robot umpires at 13 spring training ballparks hosting 19 teams
item.description: MLB has been experimenting with the automated ball-strike system in the minor leagues since 2019 but is still working on the shape of the strike zone.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Red Hat Linux to be official WSL distro
item.description: Red Hat Enterprise Linux (RHEL) is set to become an official Linux distribution for Microsoft’s Windows Subsystem for Linux (WSL), a Windows feature that allows users to run Linux environments on Windows without a separate virtual machine.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Microsoft rebrands Azure AI Studio to Azure AI Foundry
item.description: Microsoft is packaging its Azure AI Studio and other updates into a new service — Azure AI Foundry in response to enterprises’ need to develop, run, and manage generative AI applications.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: How to transform your architecture review board
item.description: I recall my angst about my first visit to an architecture review board (ARB), where a team of enterprise architects reviewed plans for new systems and application architectures.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Judge tosses Huckabee lawsuit against Meta over ads suggesting he endorsed marijuana gummies
item.description: WILMINGTON, Del. (AP) — A federal judge in Delaware on Monday dismissed a lawsuit filed by former Arkansas Gov. Mike Huckabee against social media giant Meta over advertisements using his name and image to sell CBD products.
item.dc:creator: RANDALL CHASE, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Library of Congress says an adversary hacked some emails
item.description: The library said that an adversary accessed email communications during the period from January to September.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, NewsNation
